metadata.purpose: Relative operating-cost model for comparing permissive open-source AI orchestrator candidates without depending on volatile model-provider prices.
metadata.unit_notes: Monthly operational hours are planning estimates, not vendor bills. Relative token pressure is a unitless index where 1.0 is the baseline.

coefficient_notes.review: Low human-control, CI/PR, coding-fit, and source-confidence scores increase reviewer time.
coefficient_notes.administration: Low deployment-flexibility, observability, implementation-ease, and maturity scores increase monthly ownership overhead.
coefficient_notes.governance: Low security-governance, sandbox-isolation, and observability scores increase policy and audit overhead.
coefficient_notes.failure_buffer: Low maturity, sandboxing, observability, and source confidence increase recovery and incident allowance.
coefficient_notes.token_pressure: Multi-agent and persistent-memory features can increase context/tool usage; provider portability and implementation ease reduce tuning pressure.
coefficient_notes.latency_risk: Latency risk is a relative planning score driven by token pressure, implementation friction, deployment constraints, provider constraints, and observability.
